# v8 vs S3b-local — Inner Prompt Comparison

**Date:** 2026-05-02
**Purpose:** Compare the FULL inner-LLM prompt that produced S3b-local's M1=0.07 (per-candidate, 1M-eval) against the v8 inner prompt auto-generated by the v8 meta-strategist (M1=0.01 per-candidate, 1M-eval). Identical task, identical reward, identical PPO. Whatever explains the 7× M1 gap must live in this prompt diff.

S3b-local prompt: `src/lero/prompts/v2_fewshot_k2_local/` (hand-curated, monolithic).
v8 base prompt: `src/lero/prompts/v2_fewshot_modular_v2_local/` (modular, 8 slot files).
v8 auto-authored slot: `results/lero_v8/.../prompts/v8_outer_0_seed0/guidance_observation.txt` (filled at run-time by the v8 meta-LLM).

Reference for code-level (feature-by-feature) comparison: `docs/v7_vs_s3blocal_comparison.md`. This doc is **prompt-only**.

---

## 1. The full prompts, side by side

The S3b-local prompt is a single ~80-line file. The v8 prompt is assembled at run-time from 8 slot files (one of which — `guidance_observation` — is itself written by the meta-LLM each outer iter). The render is text-concatenation.

| | **S3b-local: `v2_fewshot_k2_local`** | **v8: `v2_fewshot_modular_v2_local` (rendered)** |
|---|---|---|
| **system message (5 lines vs 3 lines)** | "You are a reward engineer designing observation enhancement functions for multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) tasks. Your objective is to create an observation enhancement function that helps agents coordinate using ONLY their LOCAL sensor data. CRITICAL: This is a RENDEZVOUS task where k=$agents_per_target agents must simultaneously occupy each target. Agents can only see via LiDAR — they do NOT know other agents' positions, target positions, or which targets are covered. Design features that help agents infer coordination state from sensor readings alone. All operations must be vectorized using PyTorch (batch dimension first). Only use torch, torch.nn.functional, math." | "You design observation enhancement and/or reward functions for multi-agent reinforcement learning. The output specification below tells you which function(s) to produce in this round. Use the state schema and constraints provided. All operations must be vectorized using PyTorch (batch dimension first). Use only `torch`, `torch.nn.functional`, and `math`." |
| **user message — Task** | (in `initial_user.txt`) "$n_agents agents must cover $n_targets targets. A target is covered when **$agents_per_target agents** are within $covering_range distance **simultaneously**. This is a RENDEZVOUS task. Arena: [-1,1] × [-1,1]." | (in `task_context.txt`, slot 1) "This is a **multi-agent rendezvous** task. $n_agents agents must cover $n_targets targets in a 2x2 arena (`[-1,1] × [-1,1]`). A target is covered when **exactly $agents_per_target agents are simultaneously within $covering_range distance** of it. Once covered, the target is removed (no respawn)." |
| **user — State schema** | "Agents observe via LiDAR sensors (rays cast in fixed directions)... `obs_state = { 'agent_pos':, 'agent_vel':, 'agent_idx':, 'n_agents':, 'n_targets':, 'covering_range':, 'agents_per_target_required':, 'lidar_targets':, $obs_lidar_agents }`" | (in `state_schema.txt`, slot 2) Same dict but explicitly with `lidar_agents` always present, and an extra paragraph: "Referencing any other key WILL raise KeyError at runtime." |
| **user — Fairness constraint** | (inline, in same block) "**You do NOT have access to:** `agents_pos`, `targets_pos`, `agents_targets_dists`, `covered_targets`, `agents_per_target`, `all_time_covered`, or any global state. Only the sensor readings above." | (in `fairness.txt`, slot 3, FROZEN by hash) "Other keys (`agents_pos`, `targets_pos`, `agents_targets_dists`, `covered_targets`, `agents_per_target`, `all_time_covered`) are not provided at runtime and will raise KeyError if referenced." |
| **user — "What you CAN infer" hints** | "Direction to nearest target / Distance to nearest target / Number of nearby targets (count of rays below threshold) / Agent density nearby (from lidar_agents, count below threshold) / Angular distribution / **Role differentiation: agent_idx as one-hot so the shared policy can assign different roles**" | **NOT PRESENT.** No equivalent block. |
| **user — guidance_shared** | n/a | (slot 4) **EMPTY by default.** Filled by meta-LLM `slot_edits` if it chooses to (rare). |
| **user — guidance_reward** | n/a | (slot 5) **EMPTY by default** for obs-only runs. |
| **user — guidance_observation** | n/a | (slot 6) **EMPTY by default** but FILLED by the v8 meta-LLM each outer iter with: a 3-paragraph operational paragraph + a complete ~10-feature `enhance_observation` fewshot in a fenced ` ```python ` block. (See §2.h below for the exact text.) |
| **user — Worked example** | A complete `def enhance_observation(...)` returning 8 features: `min_dist`, `dir_x`, `dir_y`, `n_close`, `one_hot[B,4]`. **The example INCLUDES the 4-feature agent-id one-hot.** | (in `examples.txt`, slot 7) A trivial 1-feature example: `nearest_target_dist.unsqueeze(-1)` returning `[B, 1]`. **No role one-hot. No worked LiDAR-agent feature. Anchors signature only.** |
| **user — Output spec** | "`def enhance_observation(scenario_state: dict) -> torch.Tensor: '''... Returns: [batch_dim, N] tensor where N is 4-9.''' Wrap in a ` ```python ` block." | (in `output_spec_obs_only.txt`, slot 8) Same docstring. Same `N is 4-9` range. |
| **feedback msg (subsequent iters)** | "Results from $n_candidates ... REMINDER: You can ONLY use local sensor data ... If M1 is low, think about what information the agent is MISSING that would help coordination, and how to approximate it from LiDAR readings." | "Results from $n_candidates ... Best candidate was #$best_idx. Generate $n_candidates improved versions. Carefully analyze the current reward functions and policy feedback. Keep what works, fix what doesn't." (No reminder, no "missing-info" prompt.) |

**Top-level diff:** S3b-local concentrates *task framing + inferable-hints + worked example* in a single tightly-joined block. v8 distributes them across 8 slots, drops the inferable-hints entirely, replaces the rich worked example with a trivial 1-feature anchor, and leaves the "guidance" slot for the meta-LLM to fill at run-time.

---

## 2. Aspect-by-aspect deep-dive

### 2.a — System role framing

| **S3b-local** | **v8** |
|---|---|
| `You are a reward engineer designing observation enhancement functions for multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) tasks. Your objective is to create an observation enhancement function that helps agents coordinate using ONLY their LOCAL sensor data.` `CRITICAL: This is a RENDEZVOUS task where k=$agents_per_target agents must simultaneously occupy each target. Agents can only see via LiDAR — they do NOT know other agents' positions, target positions, or which targets are covered. Design features that help agents infer coordination state from sensor readings alone.` | `You design observation enhancement and/or reward functions for multi-agent reinforcement learning. The output specification below tells you which function(s) to produce in this round. Use the state schema and constraints provided.` |

S3b-local's system message TELLS THE LLM the task is rendezvous, that k agents are needed, that it must coordinate from LiDAR alone. v8's system message is task-agnostic — it works for any LERO scenario, but doesn't pre-load the rendezvous framing.

### 2.b — Task description

| **S3b-local** (initial_user.txt §Environment) | **v8** (task_context.txt) |
|---|---|
| `$n_agents agents must cover $n_targets targets. A target is covered when **$agents_per_target agents** are within $covering_range distance **simultaneously**. This is a RENDEZVOUS task. Arena: [-1,1] x [-1,1].` | `This is a **multi-agent rendezvous** task. $n_agents agents must cover $n_targets targets in a 2x2 arena. A target is covered when **exactly $agents_per_target agents are simultaneously within $covering_range distance** of it. Once covered, the target is removed (no respawn). The challenge is implicit coordination from local sensors alone.` |

v8 actually has slightly **more** task detail (says "no respawn", spells out "implicit coordination from local sensors alone"). Not a regression here.

### 2.c — State schema (what's available)

Both list the same dict. v8 adds: `Referencing any other key WILL raise KeyError at runtime.` — slightly stronger fairness signal. Not a regression.

### 2.d — Fairness constraint (what's NOT available)

Both list the same forbidden keys (`agents_pos`, `targets_pos`, `agents_targets_dists`, `covered_targets`, `agents_per_target`, `all_time_covered`). v8's `fairness.txt` is frozen-by-hash (cannot be silently rewritten by the meta-LLM). Not a regression.

### 2.e — "What you CAN infer" hints — **THE KEY OMISSION**

| **S3b-local** | **v8** |
|---|---|
| `### What you CAN infer from LiDAR` `- Direction to nearest target: the ray with smallest value points toward the closest target` `- Distance to nearest target: min of lidar_targets rays` `- Number of nearby targets: count of rays below a threshold (e.g. < covering_range)` `- Agent density nearby: from lidar_agents, count of rays below threshold → how crowded the area is` `- Angular distribution: which directions have targets vs agents → helps decide where to go` `- **Role differentiation: agent_idx as one-hot so the shared policy can assign different roles**` | **NOT PRESENT** |

This block is the CONCEPTUAL TEACHER. It does six things at once:
- Names the spatial primitives (min, count) and gives example thresholds.
- Names the angular primitive (argmin → ray index → angle).
- Links agent density → "how crowded" — the conceptual handle for the crowding feature.
- **Names role differentiation explicitly and ties `agent_idx as one-hot` to "shared policy".**

v8 has nothing equivalent. The v8 meta-LLM is supposed to *recreate* this block in `guidance_observation` when it writes its slot text — but as §2.h shows, it doesn't recreate the role-differentiation bullet because the v8 meta-prompt doesn't ever mention agent_idx or role one-hot.

### 2.f — Worked example — **THE OTHER KEY OMISSION**

**S3b-local** (`initial_user.txt`, §Example):

```python
def enhance_observation(scenario_state: dict) -> torch.Tensor:
    lidar_t = scenario_state["lidar_targets"]       # [B, 15]
    agent_idx = scenario_state["agent_idx"]
    n_agents = int(scenario_state["n_agents"])
    B = lidar_t.shape[0]
    device = lidar_t.device

    # Direction to nearest target (which ray is closest)
    min_dist, min_ray = lidar_t.min(dim=-1)         # [B], [B]
    # Encode ray index as angle: ray_i / n_rays * 2*pi
    angle = min_ray.float() / lidar_t.shape[-1] * 2 * 3.14159
    dir_x = torch.cos(angle)                         # [B]
    dir_y = torch.sin(angle)                         # [B]

    # Count of nearby targets (within covering range)
    n_close = (lidar_t < 0.25).float().sum(dim=-1)   # [B]

    # One-hot agent index for role specialization
    one_hot = torch.zeros(B, n_agents, device=device)
    one_hot[:, agent_idx] = 1.0

    return torch.cat([
        min_dist.unsqueeze(-1),   # [B,1] — distance to nearest target
        dir_x.unsqueeze(-1),      # [B,1] — direction x
        dir_y.unsqueeze(-1),      # [B,1] — direction y
        n_close.unsqueeze(-1),    # [B,1] — nearby target count
        one_hot,                  # [B,4] — agent role
    ], dim=-1)  # [B, 8]
```

**v8** (`examples.txt`):

```python
def enhance_observation(scenario_state: dict) -> torch.Tensor:
    lidar_t = scenario_state["lidar_targets"]    # [B, n_rays]
    nearest_target_dist, _ = lidar_t.min(dim=-1) # [B]
    return nearest_target_dist.unsqueeze(-1)     # [B, 1]
```

S3b-local's worked example is a **complete answer** that already includes role one-hot as the last 4 features. Inner-LLMs are notorious for echoing the example structure: 36/36 S3b-local candidates produced role one-hot (97% rate per the consistency analysis), and the only one that didn't (`s1 iter 0 cand 1`) had M1=0.000 vs the seed's max 0.070.

v8's example is a **trivial 1-feature anchor** — its only purpose is to teach the function signature and the unsqueeze pattern. It gives zero feature-design signal. The `examples.txt` `meta.yaml` description even calls this out: *"Density of guidance is intentionally far below S3b-local's hand-curated v2_fewshot_k2_local prompt; the meta-LLM is supposed to rediscover stronger guidance through reflection."* That's the design — v8 deliberately empties the example so the meta-LLM can author a richer fewshot in `guidance_observation`. The problem is what the meta-LLM actually authors (next subsection).

### 2.g — Output specification

Both: `Returns: [batch_dim, N] tensor where N is 4-9.` IDENTICAL. (In v8 the Phase-3 run actually used the meta-LLM's authored fewshot which output 11-13 features, so the inner LLM relaxed N — the docstring is a soft hint, not a hard cap.)

### 2.h — guidance_observation (auto-authored, **only v8 has this**)

This is where the v8 meta-LLM is supposed to fill in the gap left by §2.e (no inferable-hints) + §2.f (trivial example). The actual content from outer 0:

```
Build a compact observation that helps the policy infer two things everywhere:
which target is locally most promising, and whether nearby agents make that
target already crowded. Favor dense features from lidar_targets and
lidar_agents, plus a small number of geometry summaries from own
position/velocity. Keep the representation continuous so PPO gets gradient
signal even far from cover range.

Recommended feature set: nearest target distance, target-ray mean, target-ray
std, target-ray argmin direction encoded as sin/cos, nearest-agent distance,
agent-ray mean, agent-ray std, agent-ray argmin direction encoded as sin/cos,
boundary proximity, and one mild crowding asymmetry between target and agent
densities. Optionally add one cover-zone trigger only if you want an explicit
"close enough to commit" cue.

```python
def enhance_observation(scenario_state: dict) -> torch.Tensor:
    lidar_t = scenario_state["lidar_targets"]
    lidar_a = scenario_state["lidar_agents"]
    pos = scenario_state["agent_pos"]
    vel = scenario_state["agent_vel"]
    cover_r = float(scenario_state["covering_range"])

    # DENSE: always informative
    nearest_t = lidar_t.min(dim=-1).values
    target_spread = lidar_t.std(dim=-1)
    nearest_a = lidar_a.min(dim=-1).values
    agent_spread = lidar_a.std(dim=-1)

    # Directional summaries
    t_idx = lidar_t.argmin(dim=-1)
    a_idx = lidar_a.argmin(dim=-1)
    ray_n = lidar_t.shape[-1]
    t_ang = 2.0 * torch.pi * t_idx.float() / ray_n
    a_ang = 2.0 * torch.pi * a_idx.float() / ray_n

    # Mild density/crowding signals
    t_close = (lidar_t < cover_r).float().mean(dim=-1)
    a_close = (lidar_a < cover_r).float().mean(dim=-1)
    crowd_asym = t_close - a_close

    # Own-state context
    speed = vel.norm(dim=-1)
    boundary = 1.0 - pos.abs().max(dim=-1).values

    return torch.cat([
        nearest_t.unsqueeze(-1),
        target_spread.unsqueeze(-1),
        torch.sin(t_ang).unsqueeze(-1),
        torch.cos(t_ang).unsqueeze(-1),
        nearest_a.unsqueeze(-1),
        agent_spread.unsqueeze(-1),
        torch.sin(a_ang).unsqueeze(-1),
        torch.cos(a_ang).unsqueeze(-1),
        crowd_asym.unsqueeze(-1),
        boundary.unsqueeze(-1),
        speed.unsqueeze(-1),
    ], dim=-1)
```
```

11 features. 0 mention of `agent_idx`. 0 use of `F.one_hot` or `[:, agent_idx] = 1.0`. 0 use of `n_agents` as a feature dimension. The recommended feature paragraph **lists 10 features and never mentions role differentiation**. Inner-LLMs faithfully echoed this: 0/18 v8 candidates produced role one-hot.

The outer-1 fewshot (after `switch_to_next_strategy`) is structurally similar — 12 features, also no role one-hot.

### 2.i — Feedback message (subsequent iterations)

| **S3b-local** | **v8** |
|---|---|
| `Results from $n_candidates ... Best candidate was #$best_idx. Generate $n_candidates improved versions.` `REMINDER: You can ONLY use local sensor data (lidar_targets, lidar_agents, agent_pos, agent_vel, agent_idx). Do NOT use agents_pos, targets_pos, covered_targets, or agents_per_target — those are NOT available.` `If M1 is low, think about what information the agent is MISSING that would help coordination, and how to approximate it from LiDAR readings.` | `Results from $n_candidates ... Best candidate was #$best_idx. Generate $n_candidates improved versions. Carefully analyze the current reward functions and policy feedback. Keep what works, fix what doesn't.` |

S3b-local's feedback re-anchors the inferable-hints idea ("If M1 is low, think about what information the agent is MISSING") and explicitly enumerates `agent_idx` as a usable key — a continuous nudge toward role one-hot. v8 has neither.

---

## 3. Effect on inner-LLM output (consistency over 36 vs 18 candidates)

| feature | S3b-local rate | v8 rate |
|---|---:|---:|
| **role_one_hot** | **97% (35/36)** | **0% (0/18)** |
| speed | 97% | 56% |
| crowd (any form) | 97% | 83% |
| std (lidar_t.std) | 28% | 83% |
| boundary | 25% | 89% |
| soft_prox `exp(-α·d)` | 39% | 11% |
| vel_dir (atan2 of agent_vel) | 3% | 0% |

S3b-local's prompt teaches role one-hot in TWO places (the inferable-hints bullet AND the worked example) and the rate is 97%. v8 mentions agent_idx zero times in any slot the meta-LLM can read or write, and the rate is 0%. The other features differ by ≤41 pp, but role one-hot is the standout — exactly what is theoretically required for shared-policy MAPPO with k=2 to work.

---

## 4. Why does the v8 meta-prompt generate the inner prompt this way?

The meta-LLM's instructions live in `src/lero/v8/meta_strategist.py` `_bundle_system(...)` (the Bundle Enumeration system message) and `_REFLECT_SYSTEM_V8` (the Reflection system message). Both are reproduced in summary form below.

### 4.a — Bundle enumeration system message (excerpt)

```
## What you produce
A STRATEGY BUNDLE of 3-5 full-solution hypotheses. Each strategy is a *macro*
description of what agents do (e.g. "pairs commit on shared target"), NOT a
feature list.

## Density-first feature design (v8)
The inner LLM at 1M frames learns better from FEW, DENSE features than MANY,
COVER-ZONE-GATED features. PPO needs gradient signal everywhere, not just
inside the cover zone where the agent rarely visits early.

Rules for the chosen strategy's `lero_translation_hint`:

1. Target {feature_target_min}-{feature_target_max} returned features.
   Hard cap: {feature_cap}. More features = more noise. Quality over quantity.

2. Limit cover-zone-gated features to at most {gated_cap} — features that
   are zero outside cover_r are useful as a "stay vs go" trigger but
   multiplying many of them adds no information.

3. Prefer DENSE signals: features producing informative values everywhere
   in state space:
   - mean of LiDAR rays below a threshold (still informative when nothing
     in cover zone)
   - std/variance over rays (uncertainty proxy)
   - count-asymmetry between target and agent ray-counts NORMALIZED by
     their respective totals (a continuous signed value, NOT gated by
     cover_r)
   - boundary distance from arena edge
   - directional encoding (cos/sin of argmin ray angle)

4. Include a 3-4 feature WORKING fewshot inside `lero_translation_hint`...

Anti-cheat: do NOT use S3b-local-style winning feature names (hold_signal,
approach_signal, settle_signal, etc.). Runtime grep redacts forbidden tokens.
```

### 4.b — Why this produces a guidance_observation slot WITHOUT role one-hot

Reading the meta-prompt critically, three forces conspire:

**(1) The "preferred dense signals" enumeration is a closed list of FIVE items**
   - `mean of LiDAR rays`
   - `std/variance over rays`
   - `count-asymmetry between target and agent ray-counts`
   - `boundary distance from arena edge`
   - `directional encoding (cos/sin of argmin ray angle)`

   **Role one-hot is not in the list.** It's not a LiDAR-derived feature, it doesn't fit any of the 5 categories. The meta-LLM, instructed to "prefer DENSE signals" from this exact list, never authors a one-hot block because the prompt frames the signal space as "dense LiDAR/geometry summaries" only.

**(2) The 8-12 feature target + 15 hard cap is a tight budget**
   - With 5 LiDAR primitives × 2 sensor streams (target + agent) you already get 10 features just from §4.a's preferred list.
   - Adding a 4-feature one-hot would push the count to 14 (still within hard cap) but the LLM is sensitivity-trained to the soft target band. The meta-LLM under-uses the budget on signals it wasn't reminded of.
   - Outer-0 fewshot used 11 features (right in the band) and outer-1 used 12 — both filled the budget with LiDAR/geometry summaries first.

**(3) Anti-cheat rule prevents "lifting" S3b-local handles**
   - The rule "do NOT use S3b-local-style winning feature names" prevents the meta-LLM from copying `settle_signal`/`hold_signal`/`rendezvous_pressure`. **It does NOT prevent role one-hot** (the literal pattern `F.one_hot` or `one_hot[:, agent_idx] = 1.0` is generic, not S3b-specific).
   - But the redaction grep is conservative: the meta-LLM has no way to inspect what is actually forbidden vs what is fine. So it plays safe and avoids any pattern that might overlap.

**(4) Examples slot was deliberately stripped**
   - `examples.txt` was reduced to a 1-feature trivial anchor in `v2_fewshot_modular_v2_local` (per its meta.yaml: *"density of guidance is intentionally far below S3b-local's ... the meta-LLM is supposed to rediscover stronger guidance through reflection"*).
   - The intent was: meta-LLM authors the example. The reality: meta-LLM authors *its own* example based on the §4.a closed list, so the trivial-example design choice loses the role-one-hot teaching that the original S3b-local example carried.

**(5) The reflection system message inherits the same closed list**
   - `_REFLECT_SYSTEM_V8` says: *"When trimming, prefer keeping: directional encoding (cos/sin), role one-hot, 1-2 dense density signals (mean/std), 1 cover-zone-gated decision feature, 2-3 nearest distances."*
   - Wait — **it DOES mention role one-hot!** But this guidance only fires on the `trim_features` action (when n_features > cap). In the v8 Phase-3 run, n_features was always 11-13 (under cap), so neither outer iter ever triggered `trim_features`, so neither outer iter ever surfaced this hint to the inner LLM.
   - This is the cleanest evidence: v8's meta-prompt KNOWS role one-hot matters (it's literally in the system text) but only TELLS the inner-LLM about it when overflowing the budget. The "happy path" (≤cap) silently drops it.

### 4.c — In one line

> v8 traded a hand-written prompt that taught role one-hot in two places (inferable-hints bullet + worked example) for a meta-LLM-authored prompt that enumerates a closed list of 5 dense-LiDAR signals that doesn't include role one-hot, with the only system-prompt mention of role one-hot gated behind a `trim_features` reflection action that never triggers when the inner LLM stays under cap.

### 4.d — Minimal fix

Add `role_one_hot` to the §4.a "preferred dense signals" enumeration AND to the bundle-system "Rules for the chosen strategy's `lero_translation_hint`" as a mandatory sixth item:

> *"6. **Mandatory: include a 4-feature agent-id one-hot** (`F.one_hot(agent_idx, n_agents)`). Shared-policy MAPPO needs role differentiation. The 4 dims count toward the budget; raise the upper target to 12-15 and the cap to 18 to fit them comfortably."*

Predicted effect: inner-LLM role-one-hot rate jumps from 0% to ≥80% (matching S3b-local's 97%), unlocking the per-candidate M1 from ~0.003 mean toward S3b-local's 0.022 mean. No other prompt changes required.
